EO_Leg_ID: Senate Bill 437/Act 945 of the 2025 Regular Session/The Arkansas Wind Energy Development Act

action_tool_name: Establishing Procedures for Certified Recovery Residences
reform_category: Shift Culture,Set Projects up for Success,Promote Certainty in Permitting Process
description:
Florida Chapter 2025-182 requires counties and municipalities to adopt an ordinance establishing procedures for the review and approval of certified recovery residences, including a process for requesting reasonable accommodations from land use regulations that otherwise prohibit them. It sets requirements for these ordinances with the intent to "simplify the establishment and maintenance of a recovery residence".

Recovery residences are alcohol- and drug-free living environments for individuals in recovery from alcohol and drugs. Florida has a voluntary certification program for recovery residences.

action_tool_name: Allowing Self-Certification and At-Risk Submittals for Preliminary Work
reform_category: Promote Certainty in Permitting Process,Consolidate or Eliminate Steps
description: Arizona allows qualified professionals to self‑certify and enabling at‑risk submittals for preliminary work give applicants more control over timelines and reduce unpredictability from sequential reviews, especially for compliant developers. This lets projects advance certain preparatory activities while final approvals are pending under defined conditions.

action_tool_name: Establishing State Permitting Requirements for Carbon Capture Storage
reform_category: Change Decision-Making Level
description: The SAFE CCS Act turns carbon capture and storage (CCS) into a state‑permitted activity, layered on top of federal Class VI well permitting, and gives Illinois EPA an ongoing role over deciding where, when, and how injection can occur.
EO_Leg_ID: Public Act 103-0651 - IL SAFE CCS Act

action_tool_name: Updating frameworks and exemptions
reform_category: Promote Certainty in Permitting Process,Shift Culture,Consolidate or Eliminate Steps
description:
- Vermont temporarily loosened Act 250 review for some housing projects through 2027, especially in places the state wants to encourage growth. 
- Outlined the long-term tiered Act 250 jurisdiction framework for Tiers 1A/1B, 2, and 3, shifting from a one-size-fits-all approach to a map-based system with different rules depending on the area/tiers. 
    - **Tier 1A/1B**: places where growth is encouraged and Act 250 review will be limited or removed.
    - **Tier 2**: the middle category, where existing Act 250 thresholds still mostly apply, with some added rules.
    - **Tier 3**: sensitive areas with stronger protections, especially for critical natural resources.
- Required future land use maps to use consistent land use categories tied to state designation and Act 250 jurisdictional areas.

action_tool_name: Prioritizing Siting Approvals for Projects that Had to Begin Construction Before the Federal Deadline.
reform_category: Shift Culture,Consolidate or Eliminate Steps
description: A 2025 Executive Order directed the Oregon Department of Energy to eliminate unnecessary federal agency consultation delays whenever Oregon law does not explicitly mandate them, streamlining the permitting timeline for time-sensitive renewable projects. This "cut red-tape wherever legally possible" approach allows state agencies to bypass federal coordination processes that aren't required by Oregon statute, compressing what could take months into weeks. The exemption applies only where state law permits agencies to proceed independently, ensuring legal compliance while accelerating approval speeds for projects racing against the federal deadline.

action_tool_name: Studying the Impact of Data Centers on Groundwater Supply
reform_category: Set Projects up for Success,Benefits for Nature/Communities
description: A 2024 VA House resolution requested that the Department of Environmental Quality complete a one-year study of the groundwater supply in the Commonwealth with technical assistance provided by the State Water Control Board. The Department shall complete its meetings by November 30, 2025, and submit to the Governor and the General Assembly an executive summary and a report of its findings and recommendations no later than the first day of the 2026 Regular Session of the General Assembly.

action_tool_name: Creating the PermiTrack Digital Highway Work Permit System
reform_category: Utilize Data and Technology
description: Creates a new digital permitting platform for highway work permits replacing the paper-based system. Provides online application submission, permit tracking, document upload, and real-time status monitoring for utility, construction, and right-of-way permits on state highways.

action_tool_name: Amending Housing and Transit Reinvestment Zone Framework to Establish Vested Development Rights
reform_category: Promote Certainty in Permitting Process,Change Decision-Making Level,Set Projects up for Success
description:
This law operates primarily as an accountability mechanism for county governments that failed to comply with the prior year's requirement to submit Housing and Transit Reinvestment Zone proposals. By granting vested development rights to property owners in non-compliant counties, the bill converts county inaction into a legal entitlement for qualifying developers. 
An application of the amendment was conferring specific development rights at Kimball Junction in Summit County, an instance where the state overrode local land use authority in response to county non-compliance.

action_tool_name: Streamlining Clean Energy Siting for Federal Tax Credits
reform_category: Promote Certainty in Permitting Process,Consolidate or Eliminate Steps,Change Decision-Making Level,Utilize Data and Technology
description:
EO N-33-25 directed California's state agencies to identify and prioritize projects with federal tax credit deadlines, and hence the order creates a defined state-level pathway for time-sensitive clean energy applications. 
The Energy Working Group (under the Infrastructure Strike Team) reporting framework requires agencies to document specific streamlining actions and their outcomes, which generates a tracked record of permitting reform activities. The agencies must also identify specific duplicative or unnecessary steps in siting and permitting processes that can be eliminated for qualifying projects. 
Lastly, the Order directed the Governor's Office of Business and Economic Development to engage with local permitting authorities, creating a state-to-local coordination layer for clean energy project approvals.

action_tool_name: Eliminating a 10 Year Permitting Backlog
reform_category: Shift Culture,Utilize Data and Technology
description: Beginning November 1, 2023, DEP launched a systematic effort to clear its backlog of over 2,400 overdue permit applications, investing in staff (225 new hires) and technology. By late 2025, DEP entirely eliminated the backlog for for the first time in 15 years.

action_tool_name: Modifies Department of Environmental Quality Permitting Procedures
reform_category: Consolidate or Eliminate Steps,Utilize Data and Technology,Promote Certainty in Permitting Process
description: H.B. 85 addresses procedural gaps in the Department of Environmental Quality's permitting framework by establishing application completeness standards and refining agency review authority, with the stated intent of producing more predictable review timelines. The bill functions as the statutory complement to Executive Order 2025-01, codifying in statute a subset of the permitting efficiency reforms the executive order directed agencies to evaluate.

action_tool_name: Enhancing Environmental and Public Health Protection by Creating Cooperatives between Local and State agencies
reform_category: Change Decision-Making Level,Shift Culture
description: WA Chapter 311, 2025 Laws(Ensuring Environmental and Public Health Protection from Solid Waste Handling Facility Operations) reforms the permitting of solid waste treatment facilities by requiring that the Department of Ecology (DOE) review or approve any permit submitted by local Jurisdictional Health Departments (JHDs) prior to issuance of a permit by the JHD (before this bill, the JHD would issue a permit to an applicant and send the application to DOE afterwards. The DOE may decide to appeal the permit issuance to the Pollutions Control Hearings Board (PCHB)). The bill also gives the DOE powers of enforcement if a JHD submits written notice that it defers to the DOE's authority or if the DOE determines that a JHD's enforcement action is inadequate to address violations.

action_tool_name: Delegating NEPA Resposibilities for Transportation Infrastructure Projects
reform_category: Change Decision-Making Level
description:
Governor Pillen and U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy announced an agreement through the Federal Highway Administration under which Nebraska's Department of Transportation (NDOT) assumes National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) responsibilities for transportation infrastructure projects. 

This makes Nebraska one of only a small number of states to assume NEPA responsibilities, dramatically reducing the federal review layer for NDOT highway and infrastructure projects with the goal of shortening overall environmental review timelines.

action_tool_name: Virginia DEQ Mitigation Program
reform_category: Benefits for Nature/Communities
description: DEQ provides guidance on compensatory mitigation for water quality and wetland impacts including mitigation banking.

action_tool_name: Allowing Data Centers in "Industrial Areas"
reform_category: Set Projects up for Success,Consolidate or Eliminate Steps
description: A 2025 Louisiana specifies that data centers may be included in “industrial areas” which are granted certain privileges; adds that public roads should be provided for these areas.

action_tool_name: Lowering Parking Requirements
reform_category: Set Projects up for Success,Shift Culture
description: WA Chapter 204, 2025 Laws (Concerning minimum parking requirements) lowers minimum parking requirements for multifamily units, single units and commercial spaces. It also removes parking requirements entirely for affordable housing, senior housing, child care centers, ground-level mixed-use spaces, and buildings changing use from non-residential to residential use.

action_tool_name: Updating Department Roles on Issuance of Burning Permits
reform_category: Change Decision-Making Level
description: SD Chapter 142, 2025 Laws (An Act to Update the Roles of the Department of Agriculture and Natural Resources and the Department of Public Safety Regarding Burning and Burn Permit Issuing Authority) transfers the function of approving controlled burning permits and the power to set fires in woods or prairies for the purposes of forest and range management from the secretary of Department of Agriculture and Natural Resources to the secretary of Department of Public Safety.

action_tool_name: Updating building permits and code review
reform_category: Promote Certainty in Permitting Process,Change Decision-Making Level
description:
The bill makes Maryland’s building standards apply to buildings or structures when a local jurisdiction receives a building permit application on or after August 1, 1995, and it requires the Department to keep updating those standards as new code versions are issued. It also requires local jurisdictions to implement and enforce the most current standards and any later state modifications within the stated timeframe.
It also gives local jurisdictions limited authority to grant a variance from the new-build requirements for certain net-zero-related standards if a cost-effectiveness test shows the added construction cost would exceed the social cost of the emissions reduction. For state projects, it also creates waiver processes so agencies can seek relief from high-performance building requirements when compliance is not practicable, subject to review and approval by the relevant state officials.

action_tool_name: Creating an Advisory Body to Housing and Development Project
reform_category: Change Decision-Making Level
action_tool_type: Reorganization or Creation of New Government Offices
description: EO 4, 12, and related Acts created and continued the Affordable Housing Production Task Force, a multi-agency working group of 17 members charged with developing a plan to create a one-stop shop permitting system for affordable housing projects. The working group is to fully review all rules and regulations regarding housing, permitting, and construction to identify and processes to be reformed to speed up the process.

action_tool_name: Establishing a Commission on Energy Infrastructure Siting and Permitting
reform_category: Benefits for Nature/Communities
description: Assembled a large commission of leaders representing labor, environmental justice, economic development, housing and real estate, environmental protection and land use, agriculture, local government, electric utilities, and the clean energy industry to provide recommendations to speed permitting and siting.

action_tool_name: Setting a 15 month shotclock for large clean energy projects seeking permits from the state siting board (EFSB)
reform_category: Promote Certainty in Permitting Process
description: The 2024 Climate Act creates a consolidated permitting process for large clean energy projects through the state siting board and requires permits be issued 15 months after an application is submitted and deemed complete.

action_tool_name: Prohibiting Drilling in Rural Areas of Opportunity Near National Estuarine Research Reserves
reform_category: Promote Certainty in Permitting Process,Shift Culture
description:
Florida Chapter 2025-193 prohibits drilling, exploration, or production of oil, gas, or other petroleum products in counties designated as Rural Areas of Opportunity (RAO) if the proposed site is within 10 miles of a national estuarine research reserve. 
It also requires the Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) to conduct a balancing test when granting a permit for oil and gas activities in areas near shorelines.
A RAO is a rural community designated by the Governor that presents a unique economic development opportunity or that has been adversely affected by an extraordinary economic event, distress, or natural disaster. 
The National Estuarine Research Reserve System is a network of 30 estuaries established across Florida.

action_tool_name: Removing Floor Area Ratio Cap for Residential Buildings
reform_category: Eliminate Permits/Permitting
description: Eliminates the state-imposed 12 Floor Area Ratio ceiling for residential buildings, enabling creation of higher-density zoning districts in New York City.

action_tool_name: Online Portal for Water Permit Applications
reform_category: Utilize Data and Technology,Promote Certainty in Permitting Process,Set Projects up for Success
description:
The Wisconsin DNR water permits portal is an online hub for submitting, managing, and tracking certain water-related permit applications and public notices in Wisconsin. Its purpose is to let users complete applications, sign and pay fees online, and monitor permit status through the DNR’s e-permitting system.
It also provides guidance resources like instructional videos and user guides, and it covers multiple permit categories such as water use permits, water withdrawals, and well construction notifications.

action_tool_name: Strengthening Moderate Income Housing Plan Compliance Consequences
reform_category: Shift Culture,Change Decision-Making Level,Promote Certainty in Permitting Process
description:
Chapter 36 (2025 law) strengthens the Moderate Income Housing compliance framework established by HB 462 (2022) by adding escalating daily fines ($250/day in 2024, $500/day from 2025) alongside the existing transportation funding penalty. 
The expanded strategy menu (28 or more options), increases compliance flexibility.

action_tool_name: Empowering State Agencies to Increase Permitting Certainty
reform_category: Change Decision-Making Level,Promote Certainty in Permitting Process
description:
The CRGA pairs the Illinois Commerce Commission (ICC)’s formal regulatory and adjudicatory powers with the Illinois Power Agency (IPA)’s planning and procurement authority so that permitting happens under clearer, statewide rules and with a more predictable pipeline for projects.

**ICC’s role, established by CRGA: **
- - Set and enforce statewide standards for siting and permitting of wind, solar, and storage, including limits on local restrictions and fees, and provide an administrative appeal path when developers and local governments disagree.
- - Oversee the new, statewide Integrated Resource Planning (IRP) process for utilities, using those plans to align permitting and grid upgrades with long‑term reliability and affordability goals.

**IPA’s role, established by CRGA: **
- Design and run competitive procurements (RECs, storage credits, and program carve‑outs) that determine which projects are financially viable, effectively standardizing project types, timelines, and performance requirements that then move into local and ICC‑related permitting.
- Build long‑term renewable and storage procurement plans that must be approved by the ICC, creating a feedback loop where IPA programs reflect ICC‑run IRPs and, in turn, guide which projects seek permits.
EO_Leg_ID: SB0025, 104th General Assembly - Clean and Reliable Grid Affordability Act

action_tool_name: Suspending Civil Service Exam Requirements to Fill Key Roles
reform_category: Building the Permitting Workforce
description: In 2025, Governor Hochul temporarily waived competitive civil service examination requirements for nearly all open-competitive positions, enabling state and local agencies to make permanent appointments based on minimum qualifications alone.

action_tool_name: Declaring a statewide homelessness emergency, suspending certain land use enforcement and redirecting existing funds
reform_category: Change Decision-Making Level,Shift Culture,Consolidate or Eliminate Steps,Set Projects up for Success
description: EO 23-02 declared a homelessness state of emergency in Oregon regions that had experienced at least a 50% increase in unsheltered homelessness over five years. The declaration provided two concrete mechanisms relevant to permitting and siting: it gave the state greater flexibility to override local land use enforcement for emergency shelter placement, and it redirected up to $40 million in existing Oregon Housing and Community Services funds without requiring new legislative appropriation. Each affected Continuum of Care region established a Multi-Agency Coordination (MAC) group to operationalize the response.

action_tool_name: Modifying Annual Fees for Concentrated Animal Feeding Operations
reform_category: Promote Certainty in Permitting Process
description: The South Dakota 2025 Legislative session established annual fees on any concentrated animal feeding operation (CAFO) or any operation that processes or stores manure or processes wastewater and is required to operate under a water pollution control permit. It also established a per animal annual fee for various livestock on any CAFO with a nutrient management plan, establishes the application fee for a water pollution control permit and clarifies instances where the permit application would be necessary. The 2026 legislative session passed a law to increase the fees required from these operations.

action_tool_name: Establishing the Clean Energy Siting & Permitting Division at the Department of Energy Resources
reform_category: Set Projects up for Success
action_tool_type: Reorganization or Creation of New Government Offices
description: The Division was created to provide technical support and assistance to municipalities, project proponents, and other stakeholders.

action_tool_name: Portal for Submitting Permit Applications
reform_category: Utilize Data and Technology
description: ADEM's online portal for submitting and tracking environmental permit applications, including air, NPDES/water, and underground injection control permits. Electronic submissions have been required for NOIs since December 2020.
